73.reason: [The iterator.remove() method is called on the iterator, but the variable 'last' is not updated after removal. This can cause incorrect duplicate removal logic, as 'last' still holds the previous tokenError, and subsequent comparisons may fail to detect duplicates correctly.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The iterator.remove() method is called on the iterator, but the variable 'last' is not updated after removal. This can cause incorrect duplicate removal logic, as 'last' still holds the previous tokenError, and subsequent comparisons may fail to detect duplicates correctly.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The iterator.remove() removes the current element, but the variable 'last' is not updated to the current element 't' after removal, causing the next iteration to compare the new element with the old 'last', potentially removing valid errors with the same line number incorrectly.]

836.reason: Potential infinite loop or exception if no ASSIGN or SEMICN appears before end of token list; also fails to handle function call '(' correctly if '(' is encountered before any ASSIGN, but that might be handled by the condition inside.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  Potential infinite loop or exception if no ASSIGN or SEMICN appears before end of token list; also fails to handle function call '(' correctly if '(' is encountered before any ASSIGN, but that might be handled by the condition inside.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Infinite loop risk if ASSIGN is never encountered; lacks bounds checking and fails to terminate on other valid expression/statement delimiters like RPARENT, COMMA, or RBRACE]

205.reason: Missing bounds check for index < content.length() before accessing content.charAt(index). If the comment is not closed, this will cause StringIndexOutOfBoundsException.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  Missing bounds check for index < content.length() before accessing content.charAt(index). If the comment is not closed, this will cause StringIndexOutOfBoundsException.

990.reason: Missing verification for '=' token (TokenType.ASSIGN). The code assumes 't' is '=' without checking, which causes incorrect AST construction and fails to report missing '=' errors.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  Missing verification for '=' token (TokenType.ASSIGN). The code assumes 't' is '=' without checking, which causes incorrect AST construction and fails to report missing '=' errors.

991.reason: Calls getNext() to parse ConstInitVal, effectively skipping the '=' token in the stream without validation. Should explicitly check for ASSIGN and handle errors if missing.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  Calls getNext() to parse ConstInitVal, effectively skipping the '=' token in the stream without validation. Should explicitly check for ASSIGN and handle errors if missing.

1001.reason: [For error type 'i' (missing semicolon), the specification requires the line number of the last non-terminal before the semicolon, but this returns the line of the last token which may be incorrect when the last token is not the relevant non-terminal]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [For error type 'i' (missing semicolon), the specification requires the line number of the last non-terminal before the semicolon, but this returns the line of the last token which may be incorrect when the last token is not the relevant non-terminal]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [For missing semicolon errors, this returns the line of the last parsed node, but according to spec, it should be the line where the last non-terminal before the semicolon is located. The last node may not correspond to that non-terminal in all cases]

614.reason: [getErrorLine('j', nodes) returns the line of the last node in 'nodes', which is the '(' token, but the error should be reported at the line of the last valid token before the missing ')', which might not be correctly captured here]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [getErrorLine('j', nodes) returns the line of the last node in 'nodes', which is the '(' token, but the error should be reported at the line of the last valid token before the missing ')', which might not be correctly captured here]
